Artisan Coffee House — Since 2013
Slow mornings,
deeply brewed.

Vanilla is a quiet room for coffee made with patience — small-lot beans, slow extraction, and pastries baked before the light comes in.

Scroll
Our Story

A decade spent listening to beans.

"We don't chase trends. We chase the exact ten seconds where a bean tastes like where it grew."

Vanilla began as a single roaster in a converted flower shop. The arches stayed. So did the habit of tasting every lot three times before it's allowed near a menu. Today the same discipline shapes every cup — sourced in person, roasted in small batches, poured with no rush at all.

03Direct-trade farms
12Years roasting
92°Ideal pour temp

Saturday Cupping Table

Philosophy

From cherry to cup, nothing rushed.

I.

Grown in shade

Our farms grow under native canopy, which slows ripening and deepens flavor — a choice that costs more and tastes like it.

II.

Cupped before it's chosen

Every lot is tasted blind by three roasters. Ties are broken by the person who's been here longest.

III.

Roasted in small batches

Twelve kilos at a time, so the drum never has to compromise on any single bean's schedule.

IV.

Poured to order

No urns, no batch brew sitting on a burner. Your cup starts when you sit down, not before.
